source-discipline.reason: The DR codifies the mandatory cross-check that prevents auto-overwriting BPC-author-recorded metadata when canonical disagrees. This is the procedural implementation of rule #1 source-discipline. Failure modes named (note-as-title, journal-as-title, programme-as-standard) are documented from pilot evidence rather than asserted by analogy.

best-practice-synthesis-routing.reason: DR governs evidence metadata, not BPC synthesis routing. The routing matrix referenced (V2-manual from DR-2026-05-19) is a different layer.

adversarial-research-protocol.reason: DR is methodological, not a gap closure. The integration-with-related-skills section notes that adversarial-research applies AFTER this skill clears a row; that's a sequencing observation, not a current-DR firing.

progressive-measurement-probe.reason: DR does not assert numerical specification values; it adds metadata-attribution layer beneath any future PMP-governed synthesis claim.

evidence-verification-gate-for-synthesis.reason: DR introduces a new acceptance predicate: metadata_integrity_status ∈ {OK, RESOLVED} AND verification_note carries probe timestamp. This is added to (not replacing) rule #10's existing acceptance gate. Legacy pre-DR rows are explicitly marked as needing fresh cross-check before synthesis use.

bias_direction: Self-authored DR bias: the cross-check protocol is conservative-by-design and was authored by the same Claude session that ran the three pilot batches. There is a natural pull toward formalizing the existing protocol as 'the right way' even though the 31% / 78% / 57% hold-rates across the three cohorts could reflect classifier strictness rather than only real data quality issues. Three specific guard-rails partially counterweight this: (1) the NFKD-normalizer fix surfaced one diacritic FP in batch 1 that triggered manual reclassification, showing the protocol is not invariably correct; (2) the DR's anti-patterns section names the classifier-strictness risk explicitly; (3) the acceptance criteria carve out a separate predicate (verification_note timestamp) that allows legacy rows to coexist rather than forcing back-classification of all 236 pre-DR COMPLETE rows. The remaining bias risk: future sessions may pattern-match novel ATO subtypes to one of the named MISMATCH categories rather than identifying new ones.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An independent reviewer would object that the DR is being authored from a single session's evidence without an arms-length pilot review and without owner ratification. Three batches across three cohorts is the minimum threshold per DR-2026-05-19 step 5 precedent, but it is the floor of that threshold, not a robust signal. The reviewer would specifically push back on: (a) the truncated-DOI heuristic (slash-count + length) which catches 5 of 6 batch-1 cases but is a brittle pattern that may produce FPs on legitimate short DOIs; (b) the title-shared-token threshold of 3, which is sensitive to title length and stop-word fraction; (c) the assumption that pub_year disagreement >1 indicates wrong identifier when in fact preprint/online-ahead-of-print drift can exceed 1 year for some journals. Counter: the DR status is PROPOSED, not ratified. The owner has the floor on whether to ratify; the audit script is Level 2 (surface, not block) precisely so the protocol can be tightened without breaking the eligible-pool count. The skill file and audit script make the protocol falsifiable — future sessions can override specific decisions and the trail is preserved.
